input.character_notes: Elliot: asks factual questions when avoiding emotional ones. Petra: talks quickly under anxiety.
input.original_dialogue_issues: On-the-nose statements of emotion, identical register between characters, conclusion line instead of open question

test_cases.notes: No on-the-nose emotion statements in the rewrite. Characters clearly differentiated — Elliot stays in factual register throughout, Petra breaks into a run-on speech that reveals her self-interest without stating it. Action lines show behavior only. Notes block identifies all five major changes with specific reasoning. Scene function preserved.

input.scene_type: Job interview, protagonist is lying about his qualifications
input.character_notes: The interviewer suspects something is wrong but cannot identify what. The protagonist is performing confidence while internally panicking.
input.original_dialogue_issues: Both characters too direct — interviewer asks what she actually suspects, protagonist explains his lie to the audience through his answers.

test_cases.notes: Excellent handling of dramatic irony scene — both characters now speaking about the surface conversation (the job) while the subtext (suspicion/deception) operates entirely through what is not said. Consistent use of different register between formal interviewer and protagonist's slight over-explanation. Consistency docked: one action line used the word 'nervously' which violates the show-don't-direct-emotion principle stated in the skill.

input.scene_type: Two detectives arguing about whether to pursue an unofficial lead
input.character_notes: Senior detective: been doing this twenty years, talks in shorthand, skeptical of instinct over evidence. Junior detective: convinced she is right, overexplains when challenged.
input.original_dialogue_issues: Both detectives making full arguments, no interruption, scene reads like a debate not an argument.

test_cases.notes: Successfully introduced interruption and partial speech patterns that make the scene feel like an argument rather than a debate. Senior detective's shorthand register distinctly different from junior's overexplaining. Actionability high — notes block gave writer specific craft principles usable in other scenes. Coherence docked: the rewrite was slightly longer than the original, adding beats the original scene didn't have; the skill should have flagged this explicitly.
